name: Almodóvar del Campo and Laguna de Almod
name_original: Almodóvar del Campo a Puertollano – circular por Laguna de Almodóvar
description: This easy 12.4-mile mountain biking route from Almodóvar del Campo to Puertollano circles Laguna de Almodóvar in 1 hour 34 minutes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Almodóvar Del Campo offers a diverse landscape shaped by its volcanic heritage and expansive natural parks. The region features rolling hills, cork tree forests, and rural paths, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Notable geological formations include ancient volcanic craters and elevated hills sculpted by past eruptions. Much of the area lies within the Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona Natural Park, characterized by dryland farming, olive groves, and protected river territories. Best mountain bike trails around Almodóvar Del Campo The most popular mountain bike trail is Los Embalses de Tablillas, Montoro I y Montoro II – circular desde Puertollano, a 37.4 miles (60.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 33 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses volcanic fields and natural park landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puertollano a Argamasilla de Calatrava – circular, a moderate 17.5 miles (28.1 km) path. This route offers a journey through rural paths and varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Almodóvar del Campo and Laguna de Almod, a 12.4 miles (19.9 km) trail leading through volcanic structures and near the Laguna de Almod, often completed in about 1 hour 34 minutes. Mountain biking around Almodóvar Del Campo is defined by volcanic landscapes, rolling hills, and cork tree forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Almodóvar Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Almodóvar Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: The Almodóvar Del Campo region offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 100 routes available. These range in difficulty from easy to difficult, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: Mountain biking in Almodóvar Del Campo features a diverse landscape shaped by its significant volcanic heritage. You'll encounter rolling hills, cork tree forests, and rural paths. Many routes traverse volcanic fields and expansive natural parks, offering varied and engaging terrain.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy mountain bike trails. For a pleasant ride through volcanic structures and near a scenic lake, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042286653/almodovar-del-campo-and-laguna-de-almod" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almodóvar del Campo and Laguna de Almod&lt;/a&gt; route, which is about 19.9 km long.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers difficult routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040478122/los-embalses-de-tablillas-montoro-i-y-montoro-ii-circular-desde-puertollano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Embalses de Tablillas, Montoro I y Montoro II – circular desde Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;. This 60.2 km trail traverses volcanic fields and natural park landscapes, providing a demanding ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042269067/puertollano-a-argamasilla-de-calatrava-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertollano a Argamasilla de Calatrava – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through rural paths and varied terrain over 28.1 km. Another circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31899551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mestanza Village – Puertollano loop from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 44 km ride.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore its volcanic heritage, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable features include the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7286202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Blanca&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6470149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de Ventillas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails pass through the ecologically significant Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona Natural Park.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Almodóvar Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse volcanic landscapes, rolling hills, and cork tree forests that define the region's routes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through towns or settlements where I can find amenities?
A: Yes, some routes connect or pass near settlements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042269067/puertollano-a-argamasilla-de-calatrava-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertollano a Argamasilla de Calatrava – circular&lt;/a&gt; route starts and ends near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertollano&lt;/a&gt; and passes by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5473804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argamasilla de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, offering opportunities for breaks and refreshments.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, while winters are typically mild, though conditions can vary.

Q: Can I find viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. The trails often provide panoramic views of the Sierra de Almodóvar, the Tirteafuera depression, and the Almodóvar-Argamasilla depression. The unique volcanic formations, such as the ancient crater of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7286202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Blanca&lt;/a&gt;, also offer stunning scenic spots.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the mountain bike routes?
A: Beyond natural wonders, you can find historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de la Villa&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Almodóvar Mill&lt;/a&gt;. These offer interesting stops to learn about the local heritage while exploring the trails.
tips: In the Alcudia Valley, we find the municipality of Almodóvar del Campo built on the side of a volcano, so present in Campo de Calatrava, and whose crater is now a lagoon. The Arab footprint is the most emblematic of the city, leaving behind the memory of the Castle and above all the unique Parish of Our Lady of the Assumption. There you can see the first videomapping in Spain permanently installed in a closed space, under the theme of the life and work of San Juan de Ávila.

The Almodóvar del Campo Lagoon is a unique natural and geological feature located in the province of Ciudad Real, within the Campo de Calatrava region, famous for its volcanic landscapes.

It is a lagoon formed in the crater of a volcano after a phreatomagmatic eruption (interaction between magma and groundwater). This type of lagoon is called a maar.

It's not really an official viewpoint but it does have good views and is worth a visit if you're into these trails. The climb is not easy but it is doable and it is a good climb on a mountain bike ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Almodóvar del Campo a Puertollano circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 35 minutes to complete. This duration is based on an average pace for the 19.9 km distance and 271 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as easy, making it suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. The relatively short distance and moderate elevation gain contribute to its accessibility.

faqs.question: Where does the Almodóvar del Campo a Puertollano circular route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and begins near Almodóvar del Campo. While specific parking facilities for this exact route are not detailed, parking is generally available within the town of Almodóvar del Campo. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: Given its 'easy' difficulty rating and the presence of a lake, you can generally expect varied terrain that is not overly technical. While specific details for this exact route are not available, similar easy routes in the region often feature a mix of dirt tracks, gravel paths, and possibly some paved sections. It's designed to be accessible for mountain bikers of varying skill levels.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes by several notable points of interest. You'll experience the charm of Almodóvar del Campo, enjoy views of Laguna de Almodóvar, and potentially take in a scenic vista from the View from Cerro de San Agustín.

faqs.question: Is the Almodóvar del Campo a Puertollano circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact route are not available, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash when mountain biking, especially in natural areas or near settlements like Almodóvar del Campo and around Laguna de Almodóvar. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this route, and similar trails in the region, is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to access this specific mountain bike route. Most public trails in Spain are free to use. However, always be mindful of any local regulations or private property signs you may encounter.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly intersect with other paths. You may find yourself on parts of the Ruta de la Cuerda de la Sierra de Almodóvar, Apartadero de Calatrava, Plaza Ramón y Cajal, Calle Navas de Tolosa, Sendero El terry, and Plaza Abenojar.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends near Almodóvar del Campo, you will find various cafes, restaurants, and other amenities within the town to refuel before or after your ride.

waypoints.name: View from Cerro de San Agustín
